blocks.content:
## Preview Environments & Deployment

*From ephemeral sandbox to production URL*

Preview environments are where generated code meets reality. Master tenant isolation at scale, hot module replacement, dependency management, the preview-to-production pipeline, custom domains, and rollback as a safety primitive.

## Modules in This Level

### 3.1 Anatomy of a Preview Environment

When a user clicks Preview in Make, a cascade of infrastructure events happens in the background — and if any part of that cascade takes too long, the user sees a spinner, loses trust, and the product

### 3.2 Tenant Isolation at Scale

Multi-tenancy is the central infrastructure problem for any platform product. It is also the area where Platform PMs most frequently have to defend engineering investment to skeptical stakeholders who

### 3.3 Hot Reloading and Incremental Updates

### 3.4 Dependency Management in Ephemeral Environments

Dependency installation is the single most common source of preview latency spikes and the area where the gap between "good enough" and "production-ready" infrastructure is largest. A Platform PM who 

### 3.5 From Preview to Production Deployment

The transition from preview to production is where platform strategy becomes product strategy. Every decision here — static vs. dynamic, managed vs. DIY, instant vs. careful — shapes what kinds of app

### 3.6 Custom Domains, SSL, and the Edge
